name: Section
description: A section is a cohesive content unit expressing one idea or purpose. It serves as the primary building block of a content layout, containing specific UI components in the predesigned order. Sections in Vanilla framework are built with patterns offered as [Jinja macros](https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/templates/#macros), which are useful to you if you build sites using the [Jinja](https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/) templating engine. These macros can help abstract away some of the complexity of Vanilla's HTML, making producing complex page layouts simpler and faster.
content:
## **Composition model:**

**Structure:**

The majority of the existing patterns consist of the three main elements that help organize the content visually into one topic.

1.  **Rule:** a horizontal divider line that visually separates the section from preceding content (exception: Hero and CTA section)
2.  **Title:** in most cases mandatory text heading that defines the section's topic and helps the user to quickly scan the content. Titles use either H2 or heading muted style for Section titles and H1 for Hero titles. This area can also house (in some rare cases) supporting text elements such as a Eyebrow (label) or Subtitle.
3.  **Body:** The body is limited by the individual pattern anatomy. It is populated by Blocks and UI components. It serves as the container for the content.

**Section building elements:**

The following elements are the constituents of a section:

1.  **Section title:** introduces the content, should be concise and on point. It is a mandatory element for the majority of sections. Styled as H2 or heading muted - depending on the section.
2.  **Description:** primary text element used for the body content of a section. Optional except Newsletter sign up section. Styled always as a paragraph text.
3.  **Blocks:** repeatable structures containing parallel content units.
4.  **Block title:** text acting as an introduction to a block content or serving as a category of elements listed in the block. Uses H4, H5 and less often H3 text styling.
5.  **CTA component:** functional UI component used to attract user attention and trigger an action or navigation. It is positioned at the bottom of the content it refers to.

![Section anatomy.png](images/0b2ec6d0-b3cd-4bc6-98b7-a6368c36a1c6.png)

  

| SECTION | STATUS | TITLE | BLOCKS | ALLOWS | VARIANTS |

| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |

Logo block

| Data spotlight | Stable | Heading muted | None | Section title,Block title, Description, Link, Stat, CTA block | 2 |

| Equal heights | Stable | H2 | Divided block,

Code block | Image, Block title, Description, Link, CTA block, Button | 2 |

| Newsletter signup | Stable | H2, H3 | Logo block | Section title, Description, CTA block, Input field, Checkbox | 4 |

| Resources | Stable | H2 | Quote block | Linked title, Description, Image,Block title, Logo standalone, Author and date, Category text, Button | 3 |

List, Description, CTA block, Button | 2 |

Linked title, CTA block, Button | \\- |

Equal height block,

Article (blog) block,

Logo block,

Pricing option | List, Linked title | 2 |

![Patterns distribution.png](images/b265c7e0-9224-40b2-b359-98e1453522a3.png)

### **Content guidance:**

Use Sections to organize a narrative flow in the content units. Each Section should represent a single conceptual step in the user’s journey down the page. Use them to:

*   **To structure page content:** use Sections to divide a layout into logical topics.
*   **To build modular narratives:** use Sections to stack different content types (text, images, lists, forms) to tell a story and ensure visual separation between different parts of a page.

appliedRules.rule: Sections do not use top padding.
Bottom padding of a section is 64 px (regular) - except the last section for which it is 128 px (deep).
Hero is a mandatory first section.
Section titles can only have one of two styles: H2 or Heading muted
Section title is styled as H2 or Heading muted
appliedRules.knownExceptionForConstructs: Hero, Blog, Data spotlight, Logo section, Quote wrapper

appliedRules.rule: Section title always starts in the first column of the grid.
Larger blocks of content are placed on the right-hand side of smaller blocks.
Description text cannot be shorter than 2 lines.
Content within a layout must align to the grid columns. Content should not "hang" in the gutters (spacing between columns).
Content can occupy 25% of the available space or a multiple of that 25% (25/50/75/100)
Section padding has 4 possible values: deep (128px) / regular (64px) / shallow (24px) or none (0px).
Every tab in the tab section must have a unique name - this applies also if more than one tab section is used in the layout.
Two kinds of linked elements cannot be applied one after another - ie. linked title cannot be followed by a list of links.
